title: Smoky Crumbly Mexican Sirloin Steak Crock
description: This smoky crumbly mexican dish features sirloin steak, fermented for a unique flavor twist and served as a low-carb-friendly brunch option with oaxacan flair.

ingredients.name: 1 cup (250ml) low-fat sour cream or Greek yogurt
Fermented ingredients for added flavor (optional)
ingredients.notes: such as fermented vegetables or fruits

instructions: Slice the sirloin steak thinly against the grain.
Mix the sour cream with lime juice and salt to taste. Adjust seasoning if necessary.
Fold in grated parmesan cheese until well combined.
If using, add a tablespoon of fermented ingredients for extra depth of flavor.
Place the steak slices between two sheets of parchment paper and pound lightly with a meat mallet or rolling pin to tenderize evenly.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Place steaks in the skillet for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown, allowing juices to flow out slightly.
Remove from heat and carefully transfer to the mixture of sour cream and cheese. Mix well until all steak is coated evenly with the crumble mixture.
Serve warm as a flavorful low-carb-friendly brunch option.
